title: [Bug Report] skopeo copy OCI artifacts (Halm Chart) failed in AWS ECR (Image manifest media type is not set correctly)
body: # Issue Backgroud Hi Skopeo experts! We have met an issue when using skopeo to copy OCI artifacts (Halm Chart) between two AWS ECR Repos. In **Source** Account, the `Image manifest media type` is `application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json`, but when transferred to the **Destination** Account, the `Image manifest media type` is changed to `application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json`. You can check this with follow screenshots. ### Source OCI artifact You can see that the Artifact type is **Helm Chart**. ![source](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34271744/176460666-29203d71-c7b9-4873-8230-21d88caa7843.png) ### Destination OCI artifact But when it copied to the destination, you can see that the Artifact type is changed to **Image**. ## How to reproduce 1. Follow this guide (https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECR/latest/userguide/push-oci-artifact.html) to create an OCI artifact repo. 2. Using `Skopeo Copy` command to copy the repo to the destination AWS Account. ## Expected behavior We hope that Skopeo can copy the **Image manifest media type** for OCI artifact to the destination AWS ECR Repo as well. ## Related PR Maybe this issue is related to this PR: #1680 Hope you can fix this issue, thanks a lot!

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Please provide Skopeo version used, the exact command, and the output of that command with `--debug` (e.g. `skopeo --debug copy …`).

If you are interested in testing #1680, that would be interesting to see, but the debug log is the first step.

Hi @mtrmac , the version info and debug log is here:

### Skopeo Version
skopeo version 1.8.0 commit: 49084d2cd8c9f8e7b38ba8405d61e701d5381bc0

### Exact command
```
skopeo copy docker://123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/helm-test-chart:0.1.0 docker://222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn/helm-test-chart:0.1.0 --src-creds AWS:xxxxxxxxxxx --dest-creds AWS:xxxxxxxxxx
```

We can see that Skopeo has detected the right Content-Type:
```
DEBU[0002] Content-Type from manifest GET is "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json" 
```
But I don't know why the Content-Type changed when it upload to Chinese Region AWS ECR repo.

Yeah, that makes no sense to me. AFAICT Skopeo should be writing writing the manifest unmodified.

Can you confirm using `skopeo inspect --raw docker://$source > source` similar for the destination? Are the files byte-for-byte identical, and if not, what is the difference?

---

Separately, a `skopeo inspect --debug docker://$destination` could be useful just for the “Content-Type from manifest GET” line, to possibly notice whether the MIME type is changed on the actual registry, or just in the GUI.

### Source Raw info
Response of `skopeo inspect --raw docker://$src_domain/$IMAGE:$TAG --creds AWS:$src_cred > source`:
```json
{
    "schemaVersion": 2,
    "config": {
        "mediaType": "application/vnd.cncf.helm.config.v1+json",
        "digest": "sha256:cf7295ec1287a688be509ef27a8a0e03b9dd135d513b228001231a9759baef26",
        "size": 149
    },
    "layers": [
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.cncf.helm.chart.content.v1.tar+gzip",
            "digest": "sha256:f943720f0060517b35495f7c6295f0de68338baa2a4f678bf202fae60e60b76f",
            "size": 1471
        }
    ]
}
```
### Destination Raw info
And response of `skopeo inspect --raw docker: //$dest_domain/$IMAGE:$TAG --creds AWS:$dest_cred > dest`:
```json
{
    "schemaVersion": 2,
    "config": {
        "mediaType": "application/vnd.cncf.helm.config.v1+json",
        "digest": "sha256:cf7295ec1287a688be509ef27a8a0e03b9dd135d513b228001231a9759baef26",
        "size": 149
    },
    "layers": [
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.cncf.helm.chart.content.v1.tar+gzip",
            "digest": "sha256:f943720f0060517b35495f7c6295f0de68338baa2a4f678bf202fae60e60b76f",
            "size": 1471
        }
    ]
}
```
It seems like the files are byte-for-byte identical.

### Destination inspect --debug
And response of `skopeo inspect --debug docker://$dest_domain/$IMAGE:$TAG --creds AWS:$dest_cred > dest-debug`:
```shell
    DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf"
    DEBU[0000] Trying to access "222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn/helm-test-chart:0.1.0"
    DEBU[0000] Returning credentials for 222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn/helm-test-chart from DockerAuthConfig
    DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration
    DEBU[0000]  Using "default-docker" configuration
    DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for 222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn/helm-test-chart:0.1.0, using built-in default file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore
    DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn
    DEBU[0000] GET https://222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn/v2/
    DEBU[0000] Ping https://222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn/v2/ status 401
    DEBU[0000] GET https://222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn/v2/helm-test-chart/manifests/0.1.0
    DEBU[0000] Content-Type from manifest GET is "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json"
    DEBU[0000] Downloading /v2/helm-test-chart/blobs/sha256:cf7295ec1287a688be509ef27a8a0e03b9dd135d513b228001231a9759baef26
    DEBU[0000] GET https://222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn/v2/helm-test-chart/blobs/sha256:cf7295ec1287a688be509ef27a8a0e03b9dd135d513b228001231a9759baef26
    DEBU[0001] Returning credentials for 222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn/helm-test-chart from DockerAuthConfig
    DEBU[0001] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration
    DEBU[0001]  Using "default-docker" configuration
    DEBU[0001]  No signature storage configuration found for 222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn/helm-test-chart:0.1.0, using built-in default file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore
    DEBU[0001] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn
    DEBU[0001] GET https://222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn/v2/
    DEBU[0001] Ping https://222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn/v2/ status 401
    DEBU[0001] GET https://222222222222.dkr.ecr.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn/v2/helm-test-chart/tags/list
```
But we found that "Content-Type from manifest GET" line is `"application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json"`. 

Does this Content-Type is correct?

Yes, I think this looks as expected; so the MIME type mismatch seems to be only related to web UI view.

[In a sense, a coincidence was involved: Before #1680, `skopeo copy` would fail to copy OCI artifacts where not all layers are compressed. So this copy succeeding does not guarantee that copies of other _kinds of_ OCI artifacts will always succeed. But that’s really not related to the reported manifest MIME type mismatch.]
Thanks @mtrmac 👍, we will contact the AWS Service team to handle this web UI issue.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Yes @rhatdan , let's close this issue. It seems like already be fixed in AWS.
Thanks again.
